title: eremophila divaricata
intro: Eremophila divaricata, also known as Emu Bush or Stick Bush, is a shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its woody stem and grey-green foliage. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and stems have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Eremophila divaricata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
